roadmaps.title: Web Development
roadmaps.description: Master the modern frontend and backend ecosystem from HTML/CSS to advanced React and Node.js patterns.
roadmaps.shortDescription: Master the modern frontend and backend ecosystem from HTML/CSS to advanced React and Node.js patterns.

roadmapMetadata.title: Web Developer Complete Roadmap - Zero to Advanced
roadmapMetadata.subtitle: Ruthlessly practical. Job-oriented. No fluff. No detours.
roadmapMetadata.philosophy: Build, don't just watch. Linear sequence matters. Ship broken things.

firstPrinciples.description: Before writing a single line of code, internalize these 8 principles. They separate developers who make it from those who don't.

principles.title: Build, Don't Just Watch
principles.fullText: For every tutorial you follow, build 2 projects from scratch. Passive consumption is the #1 reason learners plateau. Your fingers must know the syntax, not just your eyes.

principles.title: Understand Over Memorize
principles.fullText: Don't memorize syntax. Understand the 'why' behind every concept. If you can explain closures or the event loop to a non-developer, you truly understand it. Understanding scales. Memorization breaks.
principles.keyTakeaway: Deep understanding enables problem-solving in unfamiliar situations

principles.title: Linear Sequence Matters
principles.fullText: You cannot learn React before JavaScript. You cannot learn Node.js before understanding async programming. The roadmap order is deliberate. Skipping ahead creates knowledge gaps that will cost you job offers.

principles.title: Ship Broken Things
principles.fullText: Your first 10 projects will be terrible. Ship them anyway. Perfectionism is procrastination in disguise. Working code beats perfect code that doesn't exist. Deploy to Vercel. Show your work.

principles.title: Google Is A Skill
principles.fullText: Senior developers Google constantly. The difference: they know exactly what to search for. Learn to read documentation, Stack Overflow, and GitHub issues. This skill is more valuable than memorizing any API.
principles.keyTakeaway: Learn to find answers, not memorize them

principles.title: The Debugger Is Your Teacher
principles.fullText: When code breaks, don't guess. Use console.log, breakpoints, and the debugger. Every error message teaches you something. The developers who debug systematically advance 10x faster than those who guess.

principles.title: Consistency Beats Intensity
principles.fullText: 2 hours every day beats 12 hours on Sunday. Coding is a muscle. Daily practice builds neural pathways. The developers who make it code every single day, even if it's just 30 minutes.

principles.title: Community Accelerates Everything
principles.fullText: Join Discord servers, Twitter/X tech communities, local meetups. Ask questions. Share your progress. The developers who learn in isolation take 3x longer. Community provides accountability, feedback, and job referrals.
principles.keyTakeaway: Learning alone is slow. Community is a multiplier.

phases.title: Foundation
phases.subtitle: HTML & CSS — THE WEB'S SKELETON
phases.description: Everything on the web is HTML and CSS. Before frameworks, before JavaScript — understand how browsers render content, how the box model works, and how to structure information semantically. This is your bedrock.

topics.name: DOCTYPE, head, body, meta tags
Semantic elements: header, nav, main, section, article, footer
Forms: input types, labels, validation attributes
Tables: thead, tbody, accessibility considerations
Accessibility: alt text, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation

learningObjectives: Understand how browsers parse and render HTML (critical rendering path)
Write semantic markup that screen readers can navigate
Build accessible forms with proper validation
Structure content for SEO and maintainability

topics.name: Selectors: element, class, ID, attribute, pseudo-classes
Specificity and the cascade — how styles are applied
Box model: content, padding, border, margin
Display: block, inline, inline-block, none
Positioning: static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky
Z-index and stacking contexts

learningObjectives: Predict exactly how any CSS rule will apply
Debug specificity conflicts without !important hacks
Calculate element dimensions including padding and borders
Position elements precisely using the right technique

topics.name: Flexbox: flex container, flex items, main/cross axis
Flex properties: justify-content, align-items, flex-grow, flex-shrink
CSS Grid: grid container, grid items, grid lines
Grid template: columns, rows, areas, gap
When to use Flexbox vs Grid — the decision framework

learningObjectives: Build any 1D layout with Flexbox confidently
Build any 2D layout with CSS Grid confidently
Choose the right layout method for any design challenge
Create complex responsive layouts without frameworks

topics.name: Media queries: syntax, breakpoints, mobile-first approach
Viewport meta tag and device pixels vs CSS pixels
Responsive images: srcset, sizes, picture element
Container queries: responsive components without media queries
Fluid typography and clamp() function

learningObjectives: Design mobile-first, then enhance for larger screens
Choose appropriate breakpoints based on content, not devices
Optimize images for different screen densities
Build components that adapt to their container size

projectsToBuild.title: Complete portfolio landing page from scratch
Clone any popular website homepage pixel-perfect
Multi-page responsive static business website

jobOutcomesUnlocked: Build any layout from a Figma design without help
Make any layout work on mobile without Googling
Explain CSS specificity and the cascade confidently
phases.milestone: Deploy a portfolio site to Netlify or Vercel

phases.title: Core Language
phases.subtitle: JAVASCRIPT — THE BRAIN OF THE WEB
phases.description: JavaScript is the only programming language of the browser. Master it deeply before touching any framework. React, Vue, Node — they are all just JavaScript. Weak JS fundamentals will haunt you in every technical interview.

topics.name: var vs let vs const — hoisting and temporal dead zone
Data types: string, number, boolean, null, undefined, symbol
Type coercion: == vs === and why this matters
Control flow: if/else, switch, ternary operator
Loops: for, while, for...of, for...in
Functions: declarations, expressions, arrow functions, scope

learningObjectives: Understand variable scoping and avoid common hoisting bugs
Write predictable comparisons using strict equality
Choose the right loop construct for any iteration task
Create reusable functions with proper parameter handling

topics.name: Arrays: map, filter, reduce, find, forEach, some, every
Array and object destructuring, spread/rest operators
Object creation, methods, shorthand syntax
Closures — the most important JS concept for interviews
The 'this' keyword in different contexts
Prototype chain and how inheritance works

learningObjectives: Transform data with array methods fluently
Explain closures and implement them in real scenarios
Predict 'this' binding in any code context
Understand prototypal inheritance vs class-based OOP

topics.name: The Event Loop — how JS handles concurrency
Callbacks and why Promises were invented
Promises: .then(), .catch(), .finally(), Promise.all()
async/await syntax — the modern standard
fetch() API for HTTP requests, JSON parse and stringify
Error handling with try/catch in async code

learningObjectives: Explain the event loop and call stack on a whiteboard
Convert callback-based code to Promise-based code
Handle multiple concurrent requests with Promise.all
Write robust async code with proper error handling

topics.name: querySelector, querySelectorAll, getElementById
createElement, appendChild, innerHTML vs textContent
Event listeners: click, submit, keydown, input, change
Event bubbling and event delegation pattern
Forms: prevent default, read values, validation
localStorage and sessionStorage

learningObjectives: Select and manipulate any DOM element efficiently
Handle user interactions with proper event management
Build interactive forms with validation
Persist data in the browser using localStorage

topics.name: Template literals, tagged templates
Modules: import/export (ESModules)
Classes: constructor, methods, inheritance, static
Optional chaining ?. and nullish coalescing ??
WeakMap, WeakSet, Map, Set data structures

learningObjectives: Write modern JavaScript using current ES2024 syntax
Organize code with ES modules for maintainability
Use classes effectively while understanding prototypes
Handle null/undefined safely with modern operators

topics.name: Console: log, error, warn, table, time, group
Setting breakpoints and stepping through code
Network tab: inspect API calls, headers, responses
Elements tab: live-edit HTML/CSS for debugging
Performance tab: identify rendering bottlenecks

learningObjectives: Debug JavaScript effectively using breakpoints
Inspect network requests and diagnose API issues
Profile and optimize page performance
Use DevTools as a daily development workflow

projectsToBuild.title: Todo App with localStorage (CRUD + filter + categories)
Weather App using fetch + real API (async/await)
JavaScript Quiz Game (DOM + state management)
Expense Tracker (arrays + localStorage + charts)

jobOutcomesUnlocked: Explain the event loop on a whiteboard
Write a debounce/throttle function from scratch
Fetch data from any API and handle all error states
Build a complete DOM app without any framework
phases.milestone: Build a working weather app and a CRUD todo app

phases.title: Frontend Framework
phases.subtitle: REACT — THE INDUSTRY STANDARD
phases.description: React powers 40% or more of all web apps. It is the most-hired frontend skill in 2025. Learn it properly — components, hooks, state management, and data fetching. This is where you become employable as a frontend developer.

topics.name: What React is and why it exists (Virtual DOM concept)
JSX: JavaScript + HTML syntax, expressions in JSX
Functional components — forget class components in 2025
Props: passing data down, default props, prop validation
Children prop and component composition patterns
Conditional rendering: &&, ternary, early return

learningObjectives: Understand React's declarative approach to UI
Write JSX and embed JavaScript expressions
Build reusable functional components
Pass data through props and compose components

topics.name: useState: local state, updater functions, state batching
useEffect: deps array, cleanup functions, rules of hooks
useRef: DOM refs and persisting values across renders
useMemo and useCallback: optimization (do not overuse)
useContext: context API to solve prop drilling
useReducer: for complex state logic, custom hooks

learningObjectives: Manage component state with useState effectively
Handle side effects and lifecycle with useEffect
Access DOM elements and persist values with useRef
Build custom hooks for reusable logic

topics.name: BrowserRouter, Routes, Route component setup
Link and NavLink for navigation
useNavigate, useParams, useLocation hooks
Nested routes and shared layout components
Protected routes with authentication guards
Dynamic routes and programmatic navigation

learningObjectives: Set up client-side routing in React applications
Create navigation with Link and handle active states
Access URL parameters and query strings
Implement protected routes for authenticated users

topics.name: Fetching on mount with useEffect (the manual way)
Loading states, error states, empty states pattern
TanStack Query (React Query) — industry standard tool
useQuery, useMutation, query invalidation pattern
Axios vs fetch — when to use each
Optimistic updates pattern for better UX

learningObjectives: Fetch data in React with proper loading/error states
Use TanStack Query for server state management
Handle mutations and cache invalidation
Choose between fetch and Axios appropriately

topics.name: CSS Modules: scoped styles, how to import and use
Tailwind CSS: utility-first, responsive modifiers (prioritize this)
shadcn/ui component library — setup and usage
Lucide icons, Radix UI accessible primitives
Styled-components/Emotion (CSS-in-JS) — know it exists

learningObjectives: Style React components with CSS Modules
Build UIs rapidly with Tailwind CSS utilities
Use shadcn/ui for accessible, customizable components
Understand the tradeoffs between styling approaches

topics.name: When do you actually need global state (honest answer)
Context API for simple global state
Zustand: lightweight modern choice (recommended)
Redux Toolkit: for larger apps and legacy codebases
Server state vs client state — the key mental model to internalize

learningObjectives: Know when to use global state vs local state
Implement Context API for simple shared state
Use Zustand for scalable state management
Distinguish between server state and client state

projectsToBuild.title: E-commerce product listing with cart (state management)
Movie search app using TMDB API (TanStack Query)
Personal finance dashboard (charts + Tailwind)
Full CRUD app with routing and protected pages

phases.milestone: START applying for Junior Frontend Developer jobs

phases.title: Backend & Databases
phases.subtitle: NODE.JS, EXPRESS & DATABASES
phases.description: Becoming full-stack means understanding servers, APIs, and databases. Node.js lets you use the JavaScript you already know on the server. Add SQL and you can build complete applications entirely end-to-end.

topics.name: Node.js architecture: event loop, non-blocking I/O
npm, npx, package.json, node_modules, .gitignore
CommonJS vs ES Modules in Node environment
Built-in modules: fs, path, http, os, events
Environment variables with dotenv (.env files)
Nodemon for development, PM2 for production

learningObjectives: Understand Node.js event-driven architecture
Manage dependencies with npm effectively
Use built-in modules for file system and HTTP operations
Configure environment variables for different stages

topics.name: REST principles: resources, HTTP methods, status codes
Express setup: routing, middleware, req/res objects
Route parameters, query strings, request body parsing
Error handling middleware (the 4-parameter signature)
CORS: what it is, why it happens, how to configure
JWT authentication: sign, verify, refresh token rotation
Input validation with Zod or Joi libraries

learningObjectives: Design RESTful APIs following industry conventions
Build Express servers with routing and middleware
Handle authentication with JWT tokens securely
Validate input data to prevent security vulnerabilities

topics.name: Relational model: tables, rows, columns, primary keys
SQL: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, WHERE clauses
JOINs: INNER, LEFT, RIGHT — when to use each type
Indexes: what they are, when to create them
Transactions: ACID properties, BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK
Database design: normalization, foreign keys, constraints
PostgreSQL setup locally and on cloud (Supabase or Neon)

learningObjectives: Write SQL queries to retrieve and manipulate data
Design normalized database schemas
Use JOINs to combine data from multiple tables
Understand transactions and ACID compliance

topics.name: Prisma: schema definition, migrations, Prisma Client
CRUD with Prisma: create, findMany, update, delete
Relations in Prisma: one-to-many, many-to-many
Drizzle ORM: SQL-like syntax, type safety (modern choice)
When to write raw SQL vs use an ORM — the honest answer

learningObjectives: Define database schemas with Prisma
Perform CRUD operations with type-safe ORM queries
Model relationships between entities
Choose between ORM and raw SQL appropriately

topics.name: Password hashing with bcrypt — never store plaintext
JWT access tokens + refresh token rotation pattern
Cookie-based sessions vs localStorage JWT tradeoffs
OAuth 2.0 flow: Login with Google implementation
Role-based access control (RBAC) patterns
HTTPS, CORS, CSRF, XSS — interview-level knowledge

learningObjectives: Implement secure password hashing with bcrypt
Design JWT authentication with refresh tokens
Integrate OAuth providers like Google
Understand and mitigate common web security threats

topics.name: Document model vs relational model differences
When MongoDB makes sense (flexible schema, rapid prototyping)
MongoDB Atlas free cloud database setup
Mongoose ODM: schemas, models, validation, queries
Aggregation pipeline for complex query operations
Honest take: PostgreSQL is more versatile for most apps

learningObjectives: Understand when to choose NoSQL over SQL
Work with MongoDB and Mongoose ODM
Design document-based schemas
Make informed database technology choices

projectsToBuild.title: Full REST API with JWT auth and PostgreSQL database
Blog platform with CMS features (complete CRUD)
Twitter/X clone backend with complex table relations

phases.milestone: Deploy a full-stack app with auth and a real database

phases.title: Modern Full-Stack
phases.subtitle: NEXT.JS, TYPESCRIPT & DEPLOYMENT
phases.description: Next.js is the dominant full-stack React framework. TypeScript is now a baseline expectation in 2025. Together with modern deployment tools, you become a production-grade engineer capable of shipping real products.

topics.name: Basic types: string, number, boolean, arrays, tuples
Interfaces vs Types — when to use each one
Generics: writing reusable typed code
Union types, intersection types, discriminated unions
Type narrowing: typeof, instanceof, in operator
Utility types: Partial, Required, Pick, Omit, Record
TypeScript with React: typing props, hooks, and events

learningObjectives: Add type safety to JavaScript code
Define complex types with interfaces and type aliases
Write generic functions and components
Use TypeScript effectively in React applications

topics.name: App Router architecture vs Pages Router (use App Router)
Server Components vs Client Components — the key distinction
File-based routing: pages, layouts, loading, error files
Server Actions: mutations without separate API routes
API Routes and Route Handlers for custom endpoints
Metadata API for SEO, dynamic metadata generation
Image optimization with the next/image component

learningObjectives: Build full-stack apps with Next.js App Router
Choose between Server and Client Components appropriately
Implement Server Actions for form submissions
Optimize images and SEO automatically

topics.name: SSR (Server-Side Rendering): when data must be fresh
SSG (Static Site Generation): for content sites and blogs
ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration): cached but revalidated
Streaming with Suspense to improve Time to First Byte
fetch() with revalidate and cache options in Next.js
Understanding Next.js complex caching layers

learningObjectives: Choose the right rendering strategy for each page
Implement ISR for dynamic content with caching
Understand tradeoffs between SSR, SSG, and CSR
Optimize performance with proper caching strategies

topics.name: Git: branching strategies, merging, rebasing, stash
GitHub: PRs, code review, Issues, Actions for CI/CD
ESLint + Prettier: automated code quality enforcement
Husky + lint-staged: pre-commit hooks setup
Vitest and React Testing Library for unit/integration tests
Playwright or Cypress for end-to-end browser testing

learningObjectives: Use Git effectively for team collaboration
Set up CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions
Enforce code quality with ESLint and Prettier
Write tests with Vitest and React Testing Library

topics.name: Vercel: instant Next.js deployment, edge functions
Netlify, Railway, Render as solid alternatives
Environment variables management in production
Domain configuration and DNS basics
Docker: containerize any application (essential for backend)
Docker Compose: multi-service local development environments
Basic Linux CLI for remote server management via SSH

learningObjectives: Deploy Next.js apps to Vercel with zero configuration
Manage environment variables across environments
Containerize applications with Docker
Set up multi-service development environments

topics.name: NextAuth.js / Auth.js: the community standard solution
Clerk: managed auth with UI components (fastest to ship)
Supabase Auth: if you're already using Supabase
Sessions, cookies, and JWT handling in Next.js
Middleware for protecting routes at the edge
Email/password + multiple OAuth providers

learningObjectives: Implement auth with NextAuth.js or Clerk
Protect routes using Next.js middleware
Handle sessions and cookies securely
Support multiple OAuth providers

projectsToBuild.title: SaaS app with auth, Stripe payments, and dashboard
Full e-commerce (cart, checkout, orders) complete stack
Real-time chat application (WebSockets + Prisma)

phases.milestone: Apply for Full-Stack Engineer roles in the $100K+ range

phases.title: Advanced / Senior Level
phases.subtitle: SYSTEM DESIGN, PERFORMANCE & SCALE
phases.description: Senior engineers don't just write features — they design systems. This phase covers what separates mid-level from senior: performance optimization, scalable architecture, cloud infrastructure, and engineering leadership.

topics.name: Core Web Vitals: LCP, FID/INP, CLS — measure and optimize
Code splitting, lazy loading, dynamic imports
Bundle analysis with webpack-bundle-analyzer
Image optimization: WebP, AVIF, responsive images
Caching strategies: HTTP cache, CDN, service workers
Database query optimization: EXPLAIN ANALYZE, N+1 problem
Redis for caching, sessions, and pub/sub

learningObjectives: Measure and optimize Core Web Vitals
Implement code splitting and lazy loading
Design effective caching strategies
Optimize database queries and prevent N+1 issues

topics.name: REST vs GraphQL vs tRPC — choosing the right API style
Microservices vs Monolith — honest engineering tradeoffs
Event-driven architecture with message queues (BullMQ)
WebSockets and real-time application patterns
Database scaling: read replicas, sharding concepts
CQRS and Event Sourcing patterns
Rate limiting, circuit breakers, and bulkheads

learningObjectives: Choose appropriate architecture patterns for scale
Design APIs that can handle high traffic
Implement real-time features with WebSockets
Apply resilience patterns for fault tolerance

topics.name: AWS core services: EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, CloudFront
Infrastructure as Code with Terraform or AWS CDK
Kubernetes basics: pods, deployments, services (understand it)
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline construction
Monitoring: Sentry for errors, Datadog or Grafana+Prometheus
Log management: CloudWatch, Logtail
AWS Solutions Architect Associate cert (optional but valued)

learningObjectives: Deploy applications on AWS core services
Build CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions
Monitor applications for errors and performance
Understand container orchestration concepts

topics.name: OpenAI API, Anthropic API: text, vision, structured outputs
Vercel AI SDK for streaming AI responses in Next.js
RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) with vector databases
Embedding models and semantic search implementation
AI agents with tool use and function calling
Prompt engineering as a core product skill
Cost optimization for LLM API calls at scale

learningObjectives: Integrate LLM APIs into web applications
Implement RAG for context-aware AI responses
Build semantic search with vector embeddings
Optimize AI costs while maintaining quality

topics.name: Testing pyramid: unit, integration, end-to-end balance
Vitest: fast unit testing for modern JavaScript
React Testing Library: test behavior not implementation details
MSW (Mock Service Worker): mock APIs in test environments
Playwright: end-to-end browser automation
Test-Driven Development: when it helps and when it doesn't

learningObjectives: Design comprehensive test strategies
Write effective unit and integration tests
Mock external dependencies for reliable tests
Apply TDD where it provides value

topics.name: Writing technical design documents (RFCs)
Code review culture: giving and receiving feedback well
Estimating work and communicating uncertainty to stakeholders
Mentoring junior developers effectively
Incident response, owning outages, postmortems
Open source contributions as a career accelerant

learningObjectives: Write clear technical specifications
Conduct effective code reviews
Estimate and communicate project timelines
Handle incidents with professionalism

projectsToBuild.title: Build and launch a SaaS product with real users
Contribute meaningfully to a major open-source project
Pass a system design interview: design Twitter from scratch

phases.milestone: Targeting Senior Engineer roles at $140K and above

techStack.description: Every technology you need to learn, prioritized honestly. Start with Must Learn. Add Should Learn as you progress through the phases.

technologies.name: HTML5 / CSS3
technologies.reason: You cannot web dev without these — they are the entire frontend

technologies.name: JavaScript (ES2024)
technologies.reason: The only browser language — everything else compiles to it

technologies.name: TypeScript
technologies.reason: Required at most companies in 2025, baseline expectation

technologies.name: React 19
technologies.reason: 40%+ of job listings require React experience

technologies.name: Next.js 15
technologies.reason: Dominant production framework for React applications

technologies.name: Tailwind CSS
technologies.reason: Most-used utility CSS framework in the industry in 2025

technologies.name: Node.js
technologies.reason: JS on the server — reuse your existing language skills

technologies.name: Express.js
technologies.reason: Most widely used Node.js API framework

technologies.name: PostgreSQL
technologies.reason: Most powerful open-source relational database

technologies.name: Prisma / Drizzle
technologies.reason: Type-safe database access in TypeScript projects

technologies.name: Git + GitHub
technologies.reason: Completely non-negotiable for any developer job

technologies.name: REST APIs
technologies.reason: How all web services communicate with each other

technologies.name: TanStack Query
technologies.reason: Industry standard for server state management in React

technologies.name: Zustand
technologies.reason: Lightweight, modern alternative to Redux

technologies.name: Docker
technologies.reason: Required for most backend and DevOps-adjacent roles

technologies.name: Vitest + RTL
technologies.reason: Testing is a job requirement — know the standard tools

technologies.name: Vercel / Railway
technologies.reason: Ship your portfolio projects to a real production URL

technologies.name: Supabase
technologies.reason: Fastest way to add database and auth to any project

technologies.name: tRPC
technologies.reason: End-to-end type safety between Next.js and backend

technologies.name: GraphQL
technologies.reason: Used at larger companies such as Facebook and Twitter

technologies.name: Redis
technologies.reason: Caching, session storage, and job queue management

technologies.name: AWS Core Services
technologies.reason: Most enterprise-scale applications live on AWS

technologies.name: Kubernetes
technologies.reason: Needed at scale and for dedicated DevOps roles

technologies.name: MongoDB
technologies.reason: Many legacy and startup codebases still use it

timeline.title: Realistic Month-by-Month Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Exactly when to learn what — and precisely when to start applying for jobs at each stage.

monthlyBreakdown.description: Master semantic HTML, the full box model, Flexbox, Grid, and media queries. By end of month 2 you should be able to clone any website layout from a Figma design completely on your own.
monthlyBreakdown.milestone: Deploy a portfolio site to Netlify or Vercel

monthlyBreakdown.description: JS basics, DOM manipulation, async/await. Build real projects that fetch real APIs. This is the hardest phase — the mental models are genuinely difficult. Push through. It will click eventually.
monthlyBreakdown.milestone: Build a working weather app and a CRUD todo app

monthlyBreakdown.description: Move from vanilla JS to component-based thinking. Build 3 to 4 significant React apps. Start applying to junior frontend roles in month 6 — you are genuinely employable at this point.
monthlyBreakdown.milestone: START applying for Junior Frontend Developer jobs

monthlyBreakdown.description: Build your first full-stack application. REST API with authentication connected to a real database. Understanding the backend makes you dramatically better at frontend work too.
monthlyBreakdown.milestone: Deploy a full-stack app with auth and a real database

monthlyBreakdown.description: TypeScript everywhere, Next.js App Router, Docker, CI/CD pipelines. Build a SaaS-level project with Stripe payments. This is where you separate from 80 percent of self-taught developers.
monthlyBreakdown.milestone: Apply for Full-Stack Engineer roles in the $100K+ range

monthlyBreakdown.description: This phase has no end date. Deepen expertise, build in public, contribute to open source, study system design every day. The gap between junior and senior is mostly time plus deliberate practice.
monthlyBreakdown.milestone: Targeting Senior Engineer roles at $140K and above

careerOutcomes.description: Salary ranges and job titles unlocked at each phase of the roadmap.

outcomes.note: Not yet job-ready — continue to Phase 2
Not yet job-ready — continue to Phase 3

finalMessage.title: THE ONLY SHORTCUT IS CONSISTENCY
finalMessage.message: There is no magic tutorial. There is no bootcamp that can skip the reps. The developers making $150K+ got there by building things, shipping them publicly, and doing it every single day.
finalMessage.learningPath: HTML → CSS → JavaScript → React → Node.js → Next.js → TypeScript → System Design → ∞
finalMessage.keyPrinciple: Forward motion beats depth paralysis. Ship broken things. Code every single day.

roadmaps.title: Data Science
roadmaps.description: Learn to analyze complex datasets, build predictive models, and visualize insights using Python and SQL.
roadmaps.shortDescription: Analyze complex datasets, build predictive models, and visualize insights.

phases.title: Python & Statistics Basics
phases.description: Foundations for manipulating data.

modules.title: Python for Data Science
modules.description: Data structures, loops, functions, and standard libraries.

topics.name: List Comprehensions
Functions & Lambdas

modules.title: Intro to Statistics
modules.description: Probability distributions, hypothesis testing, and variance.

topics.name: Descriptive Statistics
Probability Theory

phases.title: Data Wrangling
phases.description: Cleaning and preparing data for ML models.

topics.name: Numpy Arrays
Pandas GroupBy & Apply

phases.title: Machine Learning Fundamentals
phases.description: Training algorithms on datasets to predict outcomes.

topics.name: Linear Regression
Random Forests

roadmaps.title: Artificial Intelligence
roadmaps.description: Dive into deep learning, natural language processing, and computer vision with PyTorch and TensorFlow.
roadmaps.shortDescription: Deep learning, natural language processing, and computer vision.

phases.title: Math & ML Foundations
phases.description: Linear Algebra, Calculus, and core ML concepts.

modules.title: Linear Algebra & Calc
modules.description: Vectors, matrices, gradients, and partial derivatives.

topics.name: Matrix Multiplications
Gradient Descent

phases.title: Deep Learning Frameworks
phases.description: Building neural architectures.

modules.title: PyTorch Basics
modules.description: Tensors, Autograd, and building simple NNs.

topics.name: Tensor Operations
Custom Datasets

phases.title: Specialized AI Domains
phases.description: NLP and Computer Vision applications.

topics.name: Word2Vec
Attention Mechanism

roadmaps.title: Cybersecurity
roadmaps.description: Learn network security, ethical hacking, and secure software development to protect critical systems.
roadmaps.shortDescription: Network security, ethical hacking, and secure software development.

phases.title: Networking & OS Fundamentals
phases.description: How computers and protocols communicate.

modules.title: Network Protocols
modules.description: TCP/IP, the OSI Model, DNS, and Routing.

topics.name: OSI Model Layers
Packet Sniffing

modules.title: Linux Administration
modules.description: Command line, permissions, and system administration.

topics.name: File Permissions
Bash Scripting

phases.title: Offensive Security
phases.description: Learning how attackers exploit vulnerabilities.

modules.title: Web App Exploitation
modules.description: OWASP Top 10, XSS, SQLi, and CSRF.

topics.name: SQL Injection
Cross-Site Scripting

phases.title: Defensive Operations
phases.description: Protecting assets from cyber threats.

modules.title: Incident Response & Forensics
modules.description: Log analysis, malware handling, and threat hunting.

topics.name: SIEM Tools
Memory Forensics

roadmaps.title: Mobile Development
